name: BBQ Pork Lo Mein
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Bok choy, cut into 1/2 inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook noodles in plenty of boiling water until al dente, then drain and toss with sesame oil. Set aside.
2. Combine the sauce ingredients (oyster sauce, soy sauce, sugar, and pepper) in a bowl. Set aside.
3. Heat the wok, add oil, and stir-fry ginger for about 10 seconds. Add bok choy, bean sprouts, and pork; stir-fry for 2 minutes.
4. Add chicken broth, then when hot, add noodles and cover. Cook for 2 minutes.
5. Add the sauce mixture, stirring well, and serve. Variations: Instead of pork, use leftover cooked roast beef, lamb, shrimp, or chicken. Other fresh vegetables in season can be used in place of the sprouts and bok choy. From "Dim Sum" by Ruth Laws. 8 ounces lo mein noodles.
